victim.value: Murat (Joachim), former King of Naples, executed by firing squad after failed attempt to reclaim his kingdom
victim.quote: at the word "Fire!" he fell pierced by eight bullets, without a sigh, without a movement

culprit_and_motive.value: King Ferdinand and his forces, executing Murat for treason/attempting to reclaim the throne
culprit_and_motive.quote: At the death of King Ferdinand, that, head, preserved in spirits of wine, was found in a secret cupboard

resolution.value: Historical account concludes with the fates of all participants - rewards and punishments distributed
resolution.quote: Trenta Capelli was made a colonel, General Nunziante a marquis, and Luidgi died from the effects of poison

social_world.value: Military hierarchy and royal court structures - officers, generals, veterans, and Corsican hunters rallying around exiled king
social_world.quote: officers of all ranks hastened to Viscovato, veterans who had fought under him, Corsican hunters

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical evidence of decapitation discovered by carpenter - the missing head later found preserved in Ferdinand's bedroom
reveal_mechanic.quote: The carpenter pointed to a headless corpse! At the death of King Ferdinand, that head, preserved in spirits

media_and_audience.value: Public crowds gathering and crying support, transforming village into royal capital through collective belief
media_and_audience.quote: Crowds gathered at once, and cries of 'Long live Joachim!' were heard
